title: Pharma Domain Benchmark Fixture
description: Source-backed, executable benchmark fixtures for evaluating LLM performance on pharmaceutical domain tasks. Designed for dry-run execution with no paid API calls required.

provenance.summary: All fixture data is derived from publicly available, stable regulatory and reference sources. No proprietary or licensed databases are embedded. URLs cite the canonical public entry points for each source.

sources.name: FDA Drug Development and Drug Interactions Table
sources.description: FDA-maintained table of known drug substrates, inhibitors, and inducers for CYP enzymes and transporters.

sources.name: DrugBank Public Database
sources.description: Publicly accessible drug information including interactions, mechanisms, and classifications. Fixture data uses only well-established, textbook-level interaction pairs.

sources.name: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice
sources.description: International Council for Harmonisation guideline on Good Clinical Practice. Publicly available consolidated guideline.

sources.name: 21 CFR Part 312 — Investigational New Drug Application
sources.description: US Code of Federal Regulations governing IND submissions and clinical trial conduct.

sources.name: ClinicalTrials.gov
sources.description: US National Library of Medicine registry of publicly and privately supported clinical studies. Endpoint patterns in fixtures are derived from publicly listed trial designs.

sources.name: WHO ATC Classification System
sources.description: World Health Organization Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical classification of active substances.

benchmarks.name: Pharma: Drug-Drug Interaction Identification
benchmarks.description: Given a patient medication list, identify clinically significant drug-drug interactions (DDIs), classify severity, and describe the pharmacological mechanism.
benchmarks.prompt_template:
You are a clinical pharmacist reviewing a patient's medication list. For each pair of medications below, identify any clinically significant drug-drug interactions. For each interaction found, provide:

1. The interacting drug pair
2. Severity classification (Major / Moderate / Minor)
3. The pharmacological mechanism (e.g., CYP enzyme inhibition/induction, pharmacodynamic synergy)
4. Recommended clinical action

Patient medication list:
{medication_list}

Be precise about mechanisms. If you are uncertain about an interaction, state that explicitly rather than guessing.

rubric.description: Correctly identifies known interacting pairs from the fixture
Assigns severity consistent with reference classification
Describes pharmacological mechanism using correct terminology (CYP isoforms, transporters, pharmacodynamic effects)
Does not fabricate interactions that do not exist in reference sources; expresses uncertainty when appropriate

benchmarks.name: Pharma: Regulatory Guideline Comprehension
benchmarks.description: Given a regulatory scenario referencing ICH/FDA guidelines, answer structured compliance questions with specific section citations.
benchmarks.prompt_template:
You are a regulatory affairs specialist. Answer the following questions about clinical trial regulatory requirements. Cite specific guideline sections where possible.

{scenario}

Questions:
{questions}

For each answer:
1. State the requirement clearly
2. Cite the relevant guideline section (e.g., ICH E6 Section 5.x, 21 CFR 312.x)
3. Note any exceptions or conditions

If you are unsure of the exact section number, state the principle and note that the specific citation should be verified.

rubric.description: References specific guideline sections (ICH E6, 21 CFR 312, etc.)
Addresses all parts of the question including exceptions/conditions
Does not invent section numbers or requirements; acknowledges uncertainty

execution.notes: Lower temperature (0.3) recommended for pharma benchmarks to reduce variability in factual recall. Dry-run mode uses pre-built fixture responses for schema validation without API calls.

caveats: FIXTURE DATA IS NOT CLINICAL ADVICE. These benchmarks evaluate LLM text generation quality on pharma topics. They must not be used for actual clinical decision-making.

Drug interaction fixture data uses well-established, textbook-level pairs from public sources. It is NOT exhaustive and does not cover rare interactions, dose-dependent effects, or patient-specific factors.
Severity classifications in fixtures are simplified (Major/Moderate/Minor) and may not match the granularity of clinical DDI databases (e.g., Lexicomp, Micromedex).
Regulatory references cite publicly available guideline versions as of 2024-2025. Regulations change; specific section numbers should be verified against current editions.
These benchmarks test factual recall and structured reasoning, NOT clinical judgment. A high score does not indicate clinical competence.
Fixture data is derived from US/EU public regulatory sources. Requirements may differ in other jurisdictions (Japan PMDA, China NMPA, etc.).
No patient data is used. All medication lists are synthetic.
Scoring is heuristic and automated. Manual expert review is recommended for production evaluation.
The benchmark does NOT require or use any paid API endpoints. All validation runs against local fixture data.
